qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: IDOR on ext-client endpoints allows any authenticated user to read WireGuard private keys
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes an IDOR vulnerability in ext-client CRUD endpoints where SecurityCheck uses no-op permission checks. The advisory describes a JWT signature verification bypass in VerifyHostToken (logic/jwts.go) where token.Valid is not checked after jwt.ParseWithClaims. While the IDOR finding mentions JWT authentication in the exploit path, the root cause is different: no-op permission checks vs. missing signature validation. The vulnerable code locations (controllers/ext_client.go, logic/security.go) differ from the advisory's vulnerable function (logic/jwts.go).

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SQL injection in SQLite DELETE path via string-concatenated key parameter
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes a SQL injection vulnerability in database/sqlite.go where the DELETE statement is constructed via string concatenation. The advisory describes a JWT signature verification bypass in logic/jwts.go. These are completely different vulnerability types (SQL injection vs. authentication bypass), different vulnerable code locations, and different root causes.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory describes a JWT signature verification bypass in Netmaker's VerifyHostToken function (logic/jwts.go) where token.Valid is not checked after jwt.ParseWithClaims, allowing attackers to forge host tokens and read host configurations including bcrypt-hashed passwords, MQTT credentials, and WireGuard peer data. The two qualifying findings describe: (1) an IDOR vulnerability in ext-client endpoints via no-op permission checks, and (2) a SQL injection in SQLite DELETE operations. Neither identifies the VerifyHostToken signature verification flaw, the same vulnerable function/class (logic/jwts.go), or the same missing guard (token.Valid check).
evidence: Advisory vulnerability: JWT signature verification bypass in logic/jwts.go VerifyHostToken function
Advisory root cause: Missing token.Valid check after jwt.ParseWithClaims
Advisory impact: Forged host tokens allow reading host configurations with credentials
Finding 1 (idor-extclient-wg-key): IDOR via no-op permission checks in ext-client endpoints - different vulnerability type, different code location (controllers/ext_client.go, logic/security.go)
Finding 2 (sql-injection-sqlite-delete): SQL injection in SQLite DELETE - different vulnerability type, different code location (database/sqlite.go)
